checks.detail: instruction.md, task.toml, environment/Dockerfile, and tests/test.sh all exist.
solution/solve.sh, oracle.yaml, and job.yaml all present.
allow_internet=false, agent.timeout_sec=7200, verifier.timeout_sec=3600, build_timeout_sec=600, agent.user='agent'.
Dockerfile installs git and tmux via apt-get.
tests/ contains extra files (compute_reward.py, run_all.py) that should live in environment/tests/.
reasons: compute_reward.py and run_all.py present in tests/ alongside test.sh

checks.detail: Stray ANTI_CHEAT.md file at task root; no .gitignore present.
reasons: ANTI_CHEAT.md at root is not a recognized task file
no .gitignore to exclude generated artifacts

notes: Move tests/compute_reward.py and tests/run_all.py to environment/tests/ and COPY them into the image at build time; tests/ should contain only test.sh.
Remove or relocate ANTI_CHEAT.md (e.g., into docs/ or merge into README.md) so the task root contains only Harbor-recognized files.
Add a .gitignore to exclude jobs/, results/, qa-logs/, __pycache__/, and .DS_Store.

checks.detail: task.toml sets [agent] user = "agent" (line 10).
All required isolation primitives present in Dockerfile.
reasons: present: useradd agent (line 42)
present: chmod 700 /tests (lines 72, 98) plus go-rwx strip on contents (line 99)
present: chown agent:agent /solution (line 77)
present: chown -R agent:agent /app (line 83) after authoritative copies baked into /tests
present: strace installed (line 11)
present: gcc/build-essential (needed because verifier compiles agent Rust crate)
note: useradd does not pin --uid 1000 explicitly; first useradd on python:3.11-slim yields uid 1000 by default, /logs bind-mount ownership relies on this default

checks.detail: test.sh locks /logs/verifier first, wipes, runs agent code only under su agent + strace + timeout, and scores outside strace.
reasons: present: chmod 700 /logs/verifier FIRST then rm -rf (lines 39-40)
present: re-lock chmod 700 /tests after Harbor upload (line 69)
present: env sanitization unsets LD_PRELOAD/LD_LIBRARY_PATH/PYTHONPATH/BASH_ENV/CARGO_HOME etc. (lines 74-75)
present: pre-kill agent processes with survival check (lines 78-87)
present: cargo build wrapped in strace -f + su agent + env -i + timeout 3000 (lines 222-227)
present: agent rls binary executed in run_all.py via strace -f + su agent + env -i + timeout (run_all.py lines 117-123)
present: strace tripwire for reward.{txt,json} write opens on both build and per-case logs
present: scoring (diff + compute_reward.py) runs outside strace as root (lines 338-367)
present: emit_fail fallback writes reward 0 on every failure path, always exit 0
present: no chown root:root on /logs
present: relax_perms only loosens reward files after verification complete

checks.detail: runs_agent_code: cargo build executes agent build.rs and the compiled rls binary is invoked per test case.
reasons: agent code (build.rs + rls binary) runs during verification
both executions wrapped in strace -f + su agent + env -i (double defense)
scoring command (compute_reward.py) reads only static output files in WORK_DIR, never imports /app

notes: tests/ at task root contains run_all.py and compute_reward.py in addition to test.sh; guide recommends test.sh only with other assets under environment/tests/, but this does not affect isolation since /tests is locked 700 and Harbor merge-upload only overwrites by name.

checks.detail: Task ships no application dependency manifest (no Cargo.toml/pyproject/package.json); agent writes their own Cargo project from scratch.
No Python or Node packages are installed; only apt packages and rustup toolchain via shell script.
task.toml has no docker_image field set; expected during development but required before final delivery.
reasons: docker_image not set in [environment]

notes: Set [environment].docker_image to a prebuilt registry image before final delivery to pin the rustup-installed toolchain and apt package versions.

checks.detail: Deliverable, location, flags, styles, and matching criterion against GNU ls are clearly specified.
Explicit 2-hour budget with guidance to finalize binary in the last ~10 minutes.
Clear, direct, mostly colleague-style with tips and a getting-started pointer.
115 lines, just over 100 but justified by the flag/style reference tables.
Section header 'Evaluation' and phrases 'hidden suite', 'each test', 'per-case timeout' leak the existence of an evaluation harness.
reasons: '## Evaluation' heading mentions a hidden test suite comparing output to GNU ls
'Each test has a per-case timeout (10 seconds)' references the test harness directly

checks.detail: Rust, cargo, allowed crates, and fixture/example locations are documented.

notes: Rename '## Evaluation' to something like '## Output Matching' and drop 'hidden suite'/'Each test has a per-case timeout' phrasing; describe the per-invocation timeout as a general runtime guideline instead.
Consider trimming the long Output Behavior bullet list into a brief overview plus a link/pointer to a docs file to bring length under 100 lines.

checks.detail: No-op agent leaves /app/workspace without Cargo.toml, triggering not_a_rust_project fail path with score 0.0.

Filesystem locked, /tests root-only, agent code runs as 'agent' under strace with env -i and tripwires for reward writes + execve of ls/dir/vdir/oracle, prebuilt-binary candidates wiped before build, oracle hash check, anti-cheat greps; environment is restored (chmod 700 /tests, /logs/verifier wipe, pre-existing agent processes killed) before verification.
Cargo build and agent binary execution both go through 'su agent -s /bin/bash -c' so agent code never runs as root.
Both the build phase and every per-case agent invocation are wrapped in 'strace -f' with logs scanned for reward-file openat and banned execve before any scoring.
compute_reward.py uses 'python3 -ISs', reads only CLI args plus the verifier-written subscores file, and never imports or reads from /app.
No 'set -euo pipefail'; every failure path calls emit_fail and exits 0; build and per-case agent runs have explicit timeouts.
Exact byte-diff against GNU ls oracle output per test; build failure, missing binary, or wrong output all score 0.
Metric is byte-equal output vs GNU ls — any Rust implementation reproducing the quoting bytes scores equally.
Single-metric task (per-test exact-output equality) aggregated as uniform pass-rate; no multi-dimension aggregation.
Empty/constant/oracle-copy outputs cannot match the oracle's quoted output for special_names fixtures; static + strace anti-cheat blocks execve(ls/dir/vdir/oracle) and reward writes.
compute_reward.py runs outside strace and only reads a verifier-written subscores.txt; no agent code is re-executed during scoring.
Both success and failure paths write score (float), subscores (list, [] on failure), additional_data; reward.txt also written atomically and test.sh always exits 0.

Verifier scores Tier 6 quoting/escaping cases (including -lb/-lQ/-lN and a no-flag default case); all flags, quoting styles, and the shell-escape default for non-terminal output are documented in instruction.md.

All required flags, each named quoting style's semantics, byte-level LC_ALL=C interpretation, and shell-quoting rules are spelled out; -l long format relies on standard GNU ls knowledge, which is acceptable.

Rust toolchain is installed system-wide with a+rX so the non-root agent user can run cargo; /app/workspace/examples, /app/fixtures, and /app/tests referenced in instruction.md exist after Docker build.
solution/solve.sh exists, is non-empty, writes the oracle marker, and decrypts the baked GNU ls into /app/workspace/rls.
Hidden tests are structural variants of documented flag/style combinations over the fixture set described in instruction.md (special_names, basic, mixed, symlinks).
Slim base and cache cleaned, but gcc/g++/make duplicate build-essential and bc is never invoked anywhere.
reasons: gcc, g++, make are already provided by build-essential
bc is unused — compute_reward.py handles all math in Python

checks.detail: Only solve.sh present in /solution.
Most sections are justified by the strace+build-from-source threat model, but one helper is redundant.
reasons: useful: chmod 700 + wipe /logs/verifier, env scrub, pkill agent, /tests re-lock, oracle ELF validation, build-from-source enforcement, strace tripwires, static workspace greps, oracle hash check
redundant: line 71 'find /tests -type f -name *.sh -exec chmod +x' — no other .sh scripts exist in /tests (only compute_reward.py and run_all.py)

checks.detail: No individual files exceed 30MB.
Workspace contains only examples/README.md; build_tests.py runs at build time and is deleted in the same layer.

notes: Drop gcc/g++/make/bc from the apt-get line to shrink the image
Remove the unused chmod +x find on test.sh line 71

instruction_quality.task_name: Rust ls quoting and escaping clone
instruction_quality.task_summary: Implement a Rust binary at /app/workspace/rls that reproduces GNU coreutils ls name quoting and escaping. Must support -b, -q, -Q, -N, --show-control-chars, and --quoting-style across literal, shell*, c, and escape styles with byte-exact output.

measures: chmod 700 /tests re-asserted plus go-rwx stripped from non-fixture files
/app/.test_data/oracle.enc decryption key lives only in test.sh, not the image
cargo build --release executed via 'su agent' under env -i
Per-case agent binary executed via 'su agent' under env -i with PATH scrubbed
strace -f wraps build phase, log scanned for reward.{txt,json} O_WRONLY/O_RDWR/O_CREAT
strace -f wraps each agent run; tripwires for reward writes and execve of ls/dir/vdir/oracle basename
Pre-existing agent processes pkilled with retry; abort if any survive
Candidate target/release/<binname> paths wiped before cargo build
Built binary copied to root-owned BIN_DIR (agent cannot replace mid-run)
Bin name resolved from Cargo.toml [[bin]]/package metadata, not glob
Oracle ls baked into image but encrypted; system ls/dir/vdir replaced with error stubs
compute_reward.py runs outside strace, as python3 -ISs, with only --output-dir and --subscores
reward.json/reward.txt written atomically via tmp + os.replace
Static workspace anti-cheat greps for oracle path / key / reward references / banned crates / dir-vdir / hash-identical to oracle

acknowledged_vectors.vector: Agent can modify its own /logs/agent traces during agent phase
acknowledged_vectors.reason: Documented as inherent to single-container model; does not affect scoring.

acknowledged_vectors.vector: Staged fixtures under /tests/fixtures are world-readable by design
acknowledged_vectors.reason: Explicitly commented: agent already has informational copies in /app/fixtures, and the staged copy is required so the agent-UID binary can read fixtures whose modes ls -l must report.

reward_design.formula: score = sum_tier( (passed_tier / total_tier) * weight_tier )  where only tier6_quoting_escaping is in-scope with weight=1.0; gated to 0.0 on build-fail / oracle-decrypt-fail / anti-cheat / strace tripwire (cheating_penalty_multiplier=0)
reward_design.formula_explanation: Pass-rate over ~33 hidden quoting/escaping test cases scored as byte-equal-output vs GNU ls (the trusted oracle), with hard zero gates for non-Rust builds, missing binary, and reward/oracle tampering.

components.name: tier6_quoting_escaping
components.metric: fraction of cases where normalized agent stdout == normalized GNU ls stdout (trailing-whitespace stripped)
components.gate: build-from-source must succeed; no execve of ls/dir/vdir/oracle; no write-open of reward.{txt,json}; binary != oracle hash; static workspace anti-cheat clean
components.weight: 1.0 (sole in-scope tier, re-normalized from parent-task weights)

dimension_analysis.name: tier6_quoting_escaping
dimension_analysis.notes: ~33 cases spanning literal, c, escape, shell, shell-always, shell-escape, shell-escape-always quoting styles plus -b/-q/-Q/-N/--show-control-chars and -l combos across special_names + basic/mixed/symlinks fixtures; uniform per-case weight, so partial implementations earn proportional partial credit (not a gaming risk — that is intended pass-rate behavior).

redundant_items.why: build-essential already pulls these in as dependencies
no shell/python code in tests/ or solution/ calls bc; reward math is in compute_reward.py
test.sh is the only .sh under /tests; compute_reward.py and run_all.py are .py and invoked via python3
